en.extract:
The blue-bellied roller (Coracias cyanogaster) is a member of the roller family of  birds which breeds across Africa in a narrow belt from Senegal to northeast Democratic Republic of the Congo. It is resident, apart from some local seasonal movements, in mature moist savannah dominated by Isoberlinia trees.


Taxonomy
The blue-bellied roller was given the binomial name Coracias cyanogaster in 1816 by the French naturalist Georges Cuvier based on "Le Rollier à ventre bleu" that had been described and illustrated by François Levaillant in 1806. The specific epithet combines the Ancient Greek kuanos meaning "dark-blue" with gastēr meaning "belly". Levaillant mistaken believed that the specimen had been collected on the island of Java. The species is resident in West-Africa and the type location was later designated as Senegal. The species is monotypic: no subspecies are recognised.
The phylogenetic relationships among the Coracias species are shown below, from the molecular study by Johansson et al. (2018)


Description
The blue-bellied roller is a large bird, nearly the size of a jackdaw at 28–30 cm (11–12 in). It has a very dark brown back, buffy or chalky white head, neck and breast, with the rest of the plumage mainly blue. Adults have 6 cm (2.4 in) tail streamers. Sexes are similar, but the juvenile is a drabber version of the adult.
The blue-bellied roller is striking in its strong direct flight, with the brilliant blues of the wings contrasting with the dark back and cream colored head, and the tail streamers trailing behind.
The call of blue-bellied roller is a harsh clicking ga-ga-ga sound.


Distribution and habitat
This is a common bird of warm open country with some trees. These rollers often perch prominently on trees, posts, or overhead wires, like giant shrikes, whilst watching for the grasshoppers and other large insects on which they feed.


Behaviour and ecology
The display of this bird is a lapwing-like display, with the twists and turns that give this species its English name.  It nests in a hole in a tree - a tree cavity.


Status
Widespread and common throughout its large range, the blue-bellied roller is evaluated as Least Concern on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species.


In captivity
The blue-bellied roller is kept in some zoos, open air aviaries and similar educational facilities. There are instances of the bird escaping captivity and adjusting to the local environment. In Nepalganj area of Joka, Kolkata, two blue-bellied rollers have been spotted since May 2023 that have adapted to the local environment.


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet

The '''blue-bellied roller''' ('''''Coracias cyanogaster''''') is a member of the [[Coraciidae|roller]] family of  [[bird]]s which breeds across Africa in a narrow belt from Senegal to northeast Democratic Republic of the Congo. It is resident, apart from some local seasonal movements, in mature moist savannah dominated by ''[[Isoberlinia]]'' trees.

==Taxonomy==
The blue-bellied roller was given the [[binomial name]] ''Coracias cyanogaster'' in 1816 by the French naturalist [[Georges Cuvier]] based on "Le Rollier à ventre bleu" that had been described and illustrated by [[François Levaillant]] in 1806.<ref>{{ cite book | last=Cuvier | first=Georges | author-link=Georges Cuvier | year=1817 | title=Le Règne animal distribué d'après son organisation: pour servir de base a l'histoire naturelle des animaux et d'introduction a l'anatomie comparée | volume=Tome 1 | language=French | place=Paris | publisher=Déterville | page=401, Note 2 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/1844911 }} The volume has 1817 printed on the title page but was published in 1816. See: {{ cite book | last1=Dickinson | first1=E.C. | author1-link=Edward C. Dickinson | last2=Overstreet | first2=L.K. | last3=Dowsett | first3=R.J. | last4=Bruce | first4=M.D. | year=2011 | title=Priority! The Dating of Scientific Names in Ornithology: a Directory to the literature and its reviewers | location=Northampton, UK | publisher=Aves Press | isbn=978-0-9568611-1-5 | page=85 }}</ref><ref name=levaillant>{{ cite book | last=Levaillant | first=François | author-link=François Levaillant | year=1806 | title=Histoire naturelle des oiseaux de paradis et des rolliers: suivie de celle des toucans et des barbus | volume=Tome Premier | language=French | location=Paris | publisher=Chez Denné le jeune, Perlet | pages=78–79 Plate 26 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/40635040 }}</ref> The specific epithet combines the [[Ancient Greek]] ''kuanos'' meaning "dark-blue" with ''gastēr'' meaning "belly".<ref>{{cite book | last=Jobling | first=James A. | year=2010| title=The Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names | publisher=Christopher Helm | location=London | isbn=978-1-4081-2501-4 | page=127 }}</ref> Levaillant mistaken believed that the specimen had been collected on the island of [[Java]].<ref name=levaillant/> The species is resident in West-Africa and the [[type location (biology)|type location]] was later designated as Senegal.<ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Peters | editor-first=James Lee | editor-link=James L. Peters | year=1945 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=5 | publisher=Harvard University Press | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts  | page=244 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14480255 }}</ref> The species is [[monotypic]]: no [[subspecies]] are recognised.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | date=January 2021 | title=Rollers, ground rollers, kingfishers | work=IOC World Bird List Version 11.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/rollers/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=27 April 2021 }}</ref>

{{Phylogeny/Coracias}}

==Description==
The blue-bellied roller is a large bird, nearly the size of a [[jackdaw]] at {{cvt|28|–|30|cm}}. It has a very dark brown back, buffy or chalky white head, neck and breast, with the rest of the plumage mainly blue. Adults have {{cvt|6|cm}} tail streamers. Sexes are similar, but the juvenile is a drabber version of the adult.<ref name=fry>{{cite book | last1=Fry | first1=C. Hilary| last2=Fry | first2=Kathie| last3=Harris | first3=Alan | year=1992 | title=Kingfishers, Bee-eaters, and Rollers| publisher=Christopher Helm |  location=London | isbn=978-0-7136-8028-7 | pages=300–301, Plate 39 }}</ref>

The blue-bellied roller is striking in its strong direct flight, with the brilliant blues of the wings contrasting with the dark back and cream colored head, and the tail streamers trailing behind.<ref name=fry/>

The call of blue-bellied roller is a harsh clicking ''ga-ga-ga'' sound.<ref name=fry/>
[[File:Blue-bellied Roller Coracias cyanogaster Fluffed 1500px.jpg|thumb|right|Fluffing its feathers]]

==Distribution and habitat==
This is a common bird of warm open country with some trees. These rollers often perch prominently on trees, posts, or overhead wires, like giant [[shrike]]s, whilst watching for the grasshoppers and other large insects on which they feed.

==Behaviour and ecology==
The display of this bird is a [[Northern lapwing|lapwing]]-like display, with the twists and turns that give this species its English name.  It nests in a hole in a tree - a tree cavity.

==Status==
Widespread and common throughout its large range, the blue-bellied roller is evaluated as [[Least-concern species|Least Concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List]] of Threatened Species.<ref name="iucn status 19 November 2021" />

==In captivity==
The blue-bellied roller is kept in some zoos, open air aviaries and similar educational facilities. There are instances of the bird escaping captivity and adjusting to the local environment. In Nepalganj area of [[Joka, Kolkata]], two blue-bellied rollers have been spotted since May 2023 that have adapted to the local environment.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Niyogi |first1=S. |title=W African bird spotted 8k km away in Baruipur  |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/kolkata/w-african-bird-spotted-8k-km-away-in-baruipur/articleshow/102408272.cms?from=mdr |access-date=17 October 2023 |work=Times of India |date=2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Datta |first1=R. |title=African cousin of Durga's Neelkontho bird set to call Kolkata home |url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/my-kolkata/news/african-cousin-of-durgas-neelkontho-bird-set-to-call-kolkata-home/cid/1973714 |access-date=17 October 2023 |work=The Telegraph India |date=2023}}</ref>

zh.extract:
藍腹佛法僧（學名Coracias cyanogaster）是分佈在非洲由塞內加爾至扎伊爾東北部的一種佛法僧。它們是留鳥，只會有一些局部的季節性遷徙，喜歡棲息在長滿准鞋木屬的大草原。
藍腹佛法僧的體型很大，與寒鴉差不多，長約28-30厘米。它們的背部呈深綠色，頭部、頸部及胸部都呈白色，其餘的羽毛主要是藍色。成鳥的尾羽長達6厘米。雄鳥及雌鳥相似，但雛鳥的顏色較為單調。它們飛行時最為觸目。
藍腹佛法僧喜歡溫暖及廣闊的郊野，經常停留在樹上等待獵物。它們主要吃草蜢及其他大型昆蟲。
藍腹佛法僧的示愛方式像鳳頭麥雞的。它們會在樹穴中築巢。
藍腹佛法僧十分普遍及分佈很廣，故世界自然保護聯盟將它們列為無危。


參考

Fry, Fry and Harris. Kingfishers, Bee-eaters and Rollers. ISBN 0-7136-8028-8.
